name: Pixelfed
summary: Decentralized, open-source photo-sharing social network that can be used as an account-based alternative way to publish and share images across the Fediverse.

account.benefits: Users need an account on a Pixelfed instance to publish posts; limits and moderation depend on the chosen instance.

developer.notes: Pixelfed is open-source ActivityPub software. API and upload behavior are instance/application oriented rather than a simple anonymous file-host API.

allowed_file_types.notes: Pixelfed is photo/video social publishing software; upload limits and accepted media types are configured by each instance.

public_sharing.notes: Pixelfed posts and albums are designed for public/federated photo sharing, subject to post privacy and instance settings.

security.notes: Pixelfed instances run over HTTPS, but public/federated posts are not positioned as end-to-end encrypted file storage.

sources.label: Pixelfed.social about page
sources.notes: Official Pixelfed.social about page describes photo sharing, albums, comments, collections, and sign-up on an instance.

sources.label: Pixelfed GitHub repository
sources.notes: Official source repository describes Pixelfed as photo sharing for the fediverse and AGPL-licensed open-source software.

sources.label: Pixelfed docs prerequisites
sources.notes: Official docs show upload/file limits are configured at the instance/server level.

sources.label: FMHY Image Hosts
sources.notes: Seed list described Pixelfed as decentralized image sharing that requires sign-up; official sources support that classification.

primary_use.notes: Pixelfed is primarily a decentralized social network for photo/video posts rather than a normal anonymous file-host.

sharing_surface.value: Posts, albums, profiles, and ActivityPub federation
sharing_surface.notes: Sharing happens through posts, albums, profiles, and federated social distribution across instances.

max_file_size.notes: Upload size depends on the Pixelfed instance and its server configuration; official docs reference instance/server upload settings.

persistence_model.notes: Posts persist according to the selected instance's policies and the user's account/post deletion choices.

storage_model.notes: Storage is provided by each Pixelfed instance, not by a single central file-host pool.

bandwidth_model.notes: Bandwidth and delivery behavior depend on the user's selected instance and federated delivery paths.
